items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/` **Triggered by:** commit f74b0b25c (PR #58154) by `@m-redding` ## What Changed PR #58154 added: - **AZC0101** analyzer: detects `ConfigureAwait(true)` in Azure SDK library code and warns to use `ConfigureAwait(false)` instead - **ConfigureAwaitCodeFixProvider**: automatic code fixer that replaces `ConfigureAwait(true)` → `ConfigureAwait(false)` - Shared async infrastructure: `AsyncPatternAnalyzer`, `MethodBodyAnalyzer`, `AsyncAnalyzerUtilities` ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG.md** lists only `AZC0012` in Features Added — does not mention the newly-added `AZC0101` rule, its code fixer, or the shared async pattern utilities introduced in this push - **README.md** is missing the required top-level sections: *Getting started* (install, prerequisites, authenticate), *Key concepts*, *Examples*, *Troubleshooting*, *Next steps*, and *Contributing* - **README.md** does not describe how library authors can suppress AZC0101 (or other rules), nor does it provide a quick usage example showing what the analyzer flags
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/CHANGELOG.md` — edit Add an entry for AZC0101 and the code fixer under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` → `### Features Added`: ```markdown ### Features Added - Initial release of Azure.SdkAnalyzers - Added AZC0012: Avoid single word type names analyzer - Added AZC0020: Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext analyzer - Added AZC0101: Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` analyzer with automatic code fix (replaces `true` with `false`) ``` #### 1b. `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/README.md` — edit Replace the current README content with a fully structured document matching the canonical template. The README currently has a valid intro and rules table; keep those and add the missing sections around them: ```markdown # Azure SDK Analyzers Roslyn analyzers that enforce [Azure SDK Design Guidelines]((azure.github.io/redacted) for .NET library authors. This package is automatically included in all Azure SDK libraries in this repository to ensure consistent code quality and adherence to Azure SDK conventions. ## Getting started ### Install the package Install the `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` NuGet package via the .NET CLI: ```dotnetcli dotnet add package Azure.SdkAnalyzers ``` Or via the NuGet Package Manager: ```powershell Install-Package Azure.SdkAnalyzers ``` ### Prerequisites - .NET SDK 6.0 or later - A project that targets any of the supported Azure SDK target frameworks ### Authenticate the client This package contains build-time analyzers only. No authentication is required. ## Key concepts `Azure.SdkAnalyzers` provides Roslyn (compile-time) analyzers and code fixers that enforce patterns required by the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET]((azure.github.io/redacted) Diagnostics fire as warnings during `dotnet build` and are visible in the IDE editor. For the full list of rules, see [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md). ## Implemented Rules | Rule | Description | Fix | |------|-------------|-----| | **AZC0012** | Avoid single word type names | — | | **AZC0020** | Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext | — | | **AZC0101** | Do not use `ConfigureAwait(true)` | ✅ | ## Examples ### AZC0101 — ConfigureAwait(false) The analyzer flags any `await` that passes `true` to `ConfigureAwait`: ```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example // ❌ AZC0101 warning — ConfigureAwait(true) is almost always wrong in library code await task.ConfigureAwait(true); // ✅ Correct — use false to avoid capturing the caller's SynchronizationContext await task.ConfigureAwait(false); ``` A lightbulb code fix **"Use ConfigureAwait(false)"** is available in Visual Studio and VS Code to perform the replacement automatically. ### AZC0012 — Avoid single-word type names ```csharp // ❌ AZC0012 warning public class Manager { } // ✅ Use a descriptive multi-word name public class TableManager { } ``` ### AZC0020 — Propagate CancellationToken to RequestContext ```csharp // ❌ AZC0020 warning — token is dropped await client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext()); // ✅ Propagate the token await client.UpdateAsync(content, new RequestContext { CancellationToken = cancellationToken }); ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Suppressing a diagnostic Most rules can be suppressed with the standard `#pragma warning disable` / `#pragma warning restore` syntax or via a `[SuppressMessage]` attribute if the diagnostic is not configured as non-suppressible: ```csharp #pragma warning disable AZC0012 // Justified: intentionally generic name used here public class Format { } #pragma warning restore AZC0012 ``` > **Note:** AZC0101 is a **Warning** and can be suppressed. In the rare case where `ConfigureAwait(true)` is intentional, prefer omitting the `ConfigureAwait` call entirely. ### Build output shows no diagnostics Ensure the package is referenced in your `.csproj` and that the project targets a framework compatible with Roslyn analyzers (net6.0 or later is recommended). ## Next steps - Review the [Azure SDK Design Guidelines for .NET]((azure.github.io/redacted) - See [docs/list-of-diagnostics.md](docs/list-of-diagnostics.md) for detailed per-rule documentation - Browse [AZC0101.md](docs/AZC0101.md) for detailed information on the ConfigureAwait rule ## Contributing See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for contribution guidelines. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No test-backed snippets are required for this analyzer package — the examples above use plain `csharp` fences since there are no live client calls to exercise. If a snippet placeholder like `` ```C# Snippet:AZC0101Example `` is added to the README, a corresponding test file must be created: - **Test file path:** `sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/tests/Azure.SdkAnalyzers.Tests/Samples/Sample_AZC0101.cs` - Wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:AZC0101Example` / `#endregion` - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) on the method - Wrap test-only assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif` However, since these examples are of analyzer violations (not runnable code), it is acceptable to use plain `csharp` fences without snippet placeholders. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README must contain these sections in order. After applying Step 1b above, all are present: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tools` — only needed if snippet placeholders were added to README 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tools` — only needed if public types/members were added or changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.SdkAnalyzers/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [SdkAnalyzers] Docs: CHANGELOG missing AZC0101 entry and README missing standard sections
